task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully accessed the Splunk web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web UI is visible in the browser, showing the user is logged in (likely as 'admin').

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashboard titled exactly 'Authentication_Temporal_Heatmap' is visible in the Dashboards list or is currently open.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains a 2D visualization of authentication events across days and hours.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard contains a panel (heatmap, table, or 2D chart) that displays data with 'days of the week' on one axis/column and 'hours of the day' on the other. The underlying search should reference the 'security_logs' index.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the required alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An alert named exactly 'Off_Hours_Success_Alert' is visible in the Alerts list or its configuration screen is open.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert triggers on successful logins during weekends or late night hours (00:00 to 05:00).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert's search query or trigger conditions explicitly filter for successful authentications AND include logic/filters for weekends (e.g., Saturday/Sunday) or hours between 00:00 and 05:00.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert is scheduled to run hourly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert schedule settings show it is set to run hourly, either via a GUI dropdown ('Run every hour') or specifically showing the cron expression '0 * * * *'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web GUI to create the dashboard and alert.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Splunk web interface (e.g., clicking through menus, filling out alert/dashboard forms) rather than just running curl commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Splunk application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Splunk interface elements (navigation bar, Splunk logo, standard CSS/layout) are present, not a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply copy-paste the expected answers into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Configurations and queries are entered into actual Splunk search bars, dashboard panels, and alert dialogs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'Authentication_Temporal_Heatmap'. The alert must be named exactly 'Off_Hours_Success_Alert'. The target index for the data is 'security_logs'. The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!.
